summary: French Nobel laureate novelist. Known for The Immoralist, The Counterfeiters, Strait is the Gate, The Pastoral Symphony, and Lafcadio's Adventures. Key themes: homosexuality, Protestant upbringing, existential freedom.

works.name: The Immoralist
works.description: Novel about Michel, a tubercular scholar who embraces hedonism and Arab boys while his wife Marceline dies.

clues.clue: Michel contracts tuberculosis in Tunis during honeymoon; watches boy Moktir steal scissors from wife Marceline; develops interest in Arab boys
examples: the protagonist watches Moktir steal a pair of scissors from his wife Marceline and develops an interest in Arab boys

clues.clue: Michel is an archaeologist who writes 'Essay on Phrygian Religious Customs' under his father's name
examples: an archaeologist who writes a well-received 'Essay on Phrygian Religious Customs'

works.name: The Counterfeiters
works.description: Metafictional novel with a journal written to explain its own development.

clues.clue: Boris dies in a classroom when a real bullet is substituted for a fake during a botched initiation rite; Ghéridanisol convinces Boris
examples: Boris dies during an initiation ceremony when a real bullet is substituted for a fake one

clues.clue: Bernard runs away from home after finding an old love letter addressed to his mother; uses a dropped checkroom ticket to steal a bag
examples: created a character who gets a job as a secretary after using a dropped checkroom ticket to steal another man's bag

clues.clue: Gide published a Journal to explain the writing of this novel
examples: This man wrote a Journal of one of his novels to explain the development of the work

clues.clue: Alfred Jarry appears as a character
examples: Alfred Jarry, the creator of Ubu, is one of the few historical figures to appear in this French novelist's work The Counterfeiters

works.name: Strait is the Gate
works.description: Epistolary novel about Jerome's love for his highly religious cousin Alissa.

clues.clue: Alissa refuses to fulfill childhood love pact with Jerome; strives for religious purity; dies of self-imposed malady
examples: Alissa dies of a self-imposed mysterious malady after she strives for religious purity and renounces her eternal love for Jerome

clues.clue: Takes its title from Luke 13:24; Juliette's sister is Alissa
examples: It takes its title from Luke 13:24

works.name: The Pastoral Symphony
works.description: Novel about a blind girl adopted by a pastor.

clues.clue: Gertrude is a blind girl adopted by a pastor who leads her to believe the world is free from sin; after surgery she realizes his lies and falls in love with his son Jacques
examples: a pastor adopts a blind girl who he leads to believe the world is free from sin, but she attempts to kill herself after regaining her sight

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Gide's life, ideas, and literary influence.

clues.clue: Wrote Corydon defending 'pederasty'; toured Soviet Union and renounced Stalinism in Return from the U.S.S.R.
examples: 1930s tour of the Soviet Union led them to renounce their earlier sympathy for Stalinism

clues.clue: Contributed to The God That Failed (1948/49) about disillusionment with communism
examples: Gide was one of the six collaborators who included essays in this 1949 book

clues.clue: The Fruits of the Earth: 'Families, I hate you! Shut-in homes, closed doors'
examples: wrote 'Families, I hate you! Shut-in homes, closed doors' in a poem about his Protestant upbringing

clues.clue: Autobiography If It Die takes title from Gospel of John
examples: Gide's autobiography, If It Die, takes its title from a verse in this biblical gospel

comprehensive_summary:
André Gide (1869-1951) was a French novelist who won the Nobel Prize in Literature. His Protestant upbringing and homosexuality are central themes.

The Immoralist follows Michel, a tubercular scholar who embraces hedonism during his honeymoon in Tunisia. He watches the boy Moktir steal scissors from his wife Marceline and develops an interest in Arab boys. Marceline eventually dies of tuberculosis herself.

The Counterfeiters is a metafictional novel with a published Journal explaining its development. Boris is killed during a botched initiation when a real bullet replaces a fake one, orchestrated by Ghéridanisol. Bernard runs away after finding a love letter and steals a bag using a dropped checkroom ticket.

Strait is the Gate is about Jerome's love for his religious cousin Alissa, who refuses to consummate their love, strives for purity, and dies. The Pastoral Symphony concerns a pastor who adopts the blind girl Gertrude and leads her to believe the world is sinless; after surgery, she sees the truth and falls for his son Jacques.

Gide wrote Corydon defending homosexuality, The Fruits of the Earth ('Families, I hate you!'), and contributed to The God That Failed about disillusionment with communism after touring the Soviet Union.

cards.front: Novel: the tubercular Michel watches Moktir steal scissors from wife Marceline
Novel: develops an interest in Arab boys in Tunisia
Novel: Boris dies in a classroom when a real bullet replaces a fake during a botched initiation rite
Novel: Bernard runs away after finding an old love letter
Novel: steals a bag using a dropped checkroom ticket
Novel: Alissa refuses her childhood love Jerome, strives for religious purity, dies of self-imposed malady; title from Luke 13:24
Novel: a pastor adopts blind girl Gertrude, leads her to believe the world is sinless
Novel: she falls for his son Jacques after gaining sight
Author: contributed to The God That Failed about disillusionment with communism
